      "それは5ポイントのキックゴールを獲得することを特徴としています。",
      "それは最大55分の試合時間を特徴としています。"

About: It is a game that originated at and is still played at Eton College.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Eton College.
HasA: It has a playing area of 5 metres wide and 110 metres long.
It has a rugby-style scrummage called a Bully.
It has a calx area where a player can earn a shy.
It has positions such as Second, Third, Fourth, First Wall, Second Wall, Third Wall, Lines, Fly, Long, Blood Sub, and 12th Man.
It has organised sessions on Tuesdays and Thursdays in the Lent half.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a slightly curved brick wall erected in 1717.
It is characterized by a playing strip of ground 5 metres wide and 110 metres long called The Furrow.
It is characterized by a traditional and most important match played on Saint Andrew's Day.
It is characterized by the Collegers taking on the Oppidans.
It is characterized by scratch matches played throughout the Michaelmas and Lent halves.
It is characterized by the rules being first written down in 1849.
It is characterized by scoring a shy worth one point.
It is characterized by scoring a goal worth nine points.
It is characterized by scoring a kicked goal worth five points.
It is characterized by a game duration of up to 55 minutes.
HeldIn: It was held in Eton College.
HeldOn: It was held on Saint Andrew's Day.
It was held on Ascension Day.
IsA: It is one of two Eton-only codes of football played at Eton.
KnownAs: It is known as Foot-ball in an 1832 article.
KnownFor: It is known for being played on a strip of ground next to a brick wall.
LocatedIn: It is located at Eton College.
MemberOf: It is a member of Eton-only codes of football.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the season in March 1821.
PartOf: It is part of Eton-only codes of football.
PerformedBy: It was performed by boys from different year groups and masters in scratch matches.
PublishedIn: It was published in the Eton College magazine on 19 November 1832.
It was published in Bell's Life in London on 29 November 1840.
UsedFor: It is used for playing football.
It is used for moving the ball towards the opponents' end of the playing area.
UsedIn: It is used in Eton College.
UsedTo: It is used to score a shy.
It is used to attempt a goal.
It is used to pass the ball down a tunnel using knees.
WonBy: It was won by the Collegers in 1955.
It was won by the Collegers in 2022.
